ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pork trotter food product entry; no specific braised-with-mushroom variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers cooked pig trotters; no specific braised-with-mushroom-and-chestnut variant exists in FoodEx2. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for braised pig trotters with mushroom. SNOMED CT does not routinely encode composite prepared dishes at this specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of braised pig trotters with mushroom is approximately 250 g, comprising one or two trotter pieces with mushrooms, chestnuts, and braising sauce, as served in a Singaporean hawker or zi char setting.

nutrient_source.title: Braised Pig Trotters with Mushroom — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Braised Pig Trotters with Mushroom (cr_id F06100007). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat (~4.8 g/100g), potassium (~220 mg/100g), and cholesterol (~85 mg/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked pork feet (NDB 10152) as these fields are not individually reported in the HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson, Foster-Powell & Brand-Miller
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal) / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for braised pig trotters with mushroom. GI estimated at 25 (low) based on analogous mixed pork-and-vegetable braised dish with very low carbohydrate content (3.42 g/100g). The dominant carbohydrate contributors are chestnut and rock sugar; the overall dish GI is anchored by the low carbohydrate load and high protein/fat matrix which blunts glycaemic response. Reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (25 × 8.55) / 100 = 2.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 330 mg/100g from soy sauce braising liquid
hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 330 mg/100g is in the borderline zone (200–400 mg); individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g per meal and request reduced soy sauce in preparation; potassium at ~220 mg/100g and dietary fibre at 3.1 g/100g partially offset the sodium burden.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (3.42 g/100g) with high protein (17.38 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate load (3.42 g/100g) and high protein content (17.38 g/100g) support stable postprandial blood glucose; individuals with type 2 diabetes should moderate portion size to manage the high fat content (13.64 g/100g) which may contribute to insulin resistance over time, and request reduced rock sugar in the braising liquid.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat ~4.8 g/100g and cholesterol ~85 mg/100g from pork skin and collagen
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Saturated fat at approximately 4.8 g/100g and cholesterol at approximately 85 mg/100g from pork skin and collagen present a moderate cardiovascular concern; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to once per week, keep portions to 150 g, and request preparation with reduced soy sauce to lower sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat arachidonic acid load balanced by shiitake beta-glucans and anti-inflammatory spices
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Shiitake mushrooms provide beta-glucans that modulate immune response and cinnamon/ginger in the spice blend are anti-inflammatory; however, pork as a red meat is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6) which can amplify inflammatory pathways, so individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit frequency to 1–2 times per week and ensure meaningful mushroom content in the serving.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Braised Pig Trotters with Mushroom (香菇红烧猪蹄) is a classic Chinese-Singaporean claypot dish featuring whole pig trotters slow-braised with dried shiitake mushrooms and chestnuts in a rich soy-sauce-based braising liquid seasoned with rock sugar, ginger, cinnamon, and star anise. The extended braising process renders the pork skin and collagen into a gelatinous, melt-in-the-mouth texture while the shiitake mushrooms absorb the savoury-sweet braising sauce. The dish is a staple of Singaporean hawker centres and zi char restaurants, commonly served as a main dish at dinner. Nutritionally, it provides 205 kcal per 100 g, with high protein (17.38 g) from the pork, moderate fat (13.64 g) including approximately 4.8 g saturated fat from pork skin, and very low carbohydrates (3.42 g), making it suitable for low-carbohydrate dietary patterns. Dietary fibre at 3.1 g/100g comes from the mushrooms and chestnuts. Sodium at 330 mg/100g is primarily from soy sauce. The dish is clinically relevant for individuals managing type 2 diabetes due to its low glycaemic load, but requires portion moderation for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium and saturated fat content. The collagen-rich pork skin provides glycine and proline, which support joint and connective tissue health.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F06100007) with confidence 0.95. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singaporean dishes. Saturated fat, potassium, and cholesterol values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pork trotter composition data as these fields are not individually reported in the HPB record.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific braised pig trotter preparation are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F06100007) or USDA FoodData Central composite dish entries as of 2026. Data unavailability confirmed by cross-referencing USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked pork feet (NDB 10152) and USDA FoodData Central composite prepared pork dish entries, neither of which report these fields for this preparation style. Saturated fat (~4.8 g/100g), potassium (~220 mg/100g), and cholesterol (~85 mg/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked pork feet (NDB 10152) and documented as approximate values. GI of 25 is an analogous estimate based on the very low carbohydrate content of the dish (3.42 g/100g) and mixed pork-vegetable braised dish analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Dietary tags 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' present in the input record have been removed as this dish contains pork and is neither vegetarian nor plant-based. Input health_suitability condition key 'diabetes' has been mapped to 'type2_diabetes' per Veritas naming rules. Aliases consistency check: all strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array — 'Hong shao zhu ti', 'Hóngshāo zhū tí', and 'Xiānggū hóngshāo zhū tí' from aliases_by_language.romanized are present in the flat aliases array.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English name 'Braised Pig Trotters with Mushroom' is precise and unambiguous. All mandatory Singaporean-cuisine local-language names present: Chinese simplified (香菇红烧猪蹄), Chinese traditional (香菇紅燒豬蹄), Malay, Tamil, Hindi.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (205.0), protein_g (17.38), fat_g (13.64), carbohydrate_g (3.42) all present and numeric. Source is HPB Singapore Foodo (exact match, cr_id F06100007, confidence 0.95). nutrient_source fully populated.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (3.1), sodium_mg (330.0), saturated_fat_g (4.8), sugar_g (1.44) all present as numeric values. Values are plausible and supported by HPB source and USDA estimation for saturated fat.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (25, low), gi_category ('low'), glycemic_load_per_serving (2.1), gi_source fully populated. GI is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care / University of Sydney GI Database) with estimation basis documented. GL = (25 × 8.55) / 100 = 2.1 for 250g serving.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI and label for pork trotter food product provided with 'closest_parent' match type and gap note. FoodEx2 code A0CKP (pig feet, cooked) provided with 'closest_parent' match type and gap note. SNOMED CT null with documented gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. common_user_queries (10 entries), meal_context (5 entries), and ingredient_synonyms (7 entries) are comprehensive.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate), Type 2 Diabetes (low), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly. Additional condition rheumatoid_arthritis preserved from input.
